title: Old Guard

The '''Old Guard''' was a term used within the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] to describe certain [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] in the waning years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Members of the Old Guard had been or were considered as members of the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]]. Jedi considered within the Old Guard included [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], [[Sifo-Dyas/Legends|Sifo-Dyas]], [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] and [[Roan Shryne]] who was considered a peripheral member, along with many others. Windu was the only known member<ref name="Dark Lord" /> who served on the Council until his [[Death/Legends|death]].<ref name="Rots">{{Film|III}}</ref> Sifo-Dyas's views on the Jedi as a militarized force were considered too extreme and resulted in his expulsion,<ref name="TLO">{{TCW|The Lost One}}</ref> Dooku left the Order and became a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]],<ref name="AOTC">{{Film|II}}</ref> Jinn's consistent rebellion against the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]] prevented his membership<ref name="TPM">{{Film|I}}</ref> and Shryne was never made privy to the Council's decisions. Following the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]]<ref name="Dark Lord" /> in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] found mention of the Old Guard in the Jedi archives while hunting Shryne, who had survived the Purge.<ref name="Dark Lord" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Old Guard was mentioned in ''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]'', a [[2005]] novel by [[James Luceno]].<ref name="Dark Lord" />
